creator: koston@iki.fi
text:
(In reply to Kyle Evans from comment #3)

Simple counterpart to mitigate bad actors would be kern.ipc.maxpipekvaperuid, but after contemplation I chose not to suggest it, because it's a rather poor solution. In retrospect, I regret bringing up bad actors in the original bug report at all, because they represent so vastly more complex set of problems.

A malicious user has a wide range of OS services to abuse for creating a local DoS, it would be very difficult to prevent - and also mostly futile, because that would be among the best possible outcomes for a sysadmin; a malicious user volunteers themselves for removal at the cost of some downtime.
